original_abstract: Deep learning has undergone a transformative evolution, extending its reach into complex data structures like graphs. Unlike traditional Euclidean data, graphs capture intricate relational dependencies between elements, making them an indispensable format for modern machine learning tasks. This paper explores the methodology of mapping diverse applications—such as text, entities, and relational structures—into graph representations to unlock predictive insights. We provide an in-depth analysis of specialized Graph Neural Network (GNN) architectures, focusing on the mechanics of information exchange through message-passing frameworks. Specifically, we examine the technical nuances, strengths, and inherent limitations of widely adopted models, including Graph Convolutional Networks (GCNs), GraphSAGE, and Graph Attention Networks (GATs). Beyond theoretical architectural comparisons, this work surveys the broader ecosystem of GNN research, highlighting benchmark datasets, essential Python libraries, and cross-domain practical implementations. Ultimately, this paper serves as an extensive guide to the current landscape of GNNs, bridging the gap between foundational theory and real-world deployment.
humanized_abstract: Deep learning has gone through a series of revolutions and now is the time to harvest its fruits on graphs. Graphs are a class of non-Euclidean data that encodes highly complex relationships between different objects and are therefore ubiquitous in today’s machine learning applications. This post aims at giving a detailed view of the way we can transform a number of important use cases such as text, entities, and relationships into graphs and then leverage Graph Neural Networks (GNNs) to predict things. It focuses on the principles of GNNs by looking into the details of the message-passing algorithms used in them and takes a closer look at famous GNNs such as Graph Convolutional Networks (GCNs), GraphSAGE and Graph Attention Networks (GATs). It also reviews the state of the art of GNNs research along with popular datasets, libraries and implementation across multiple fields of computer science.
